perturbation.original_text: 2. CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS. Except as expressly authorized by prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall: (a) limit access to any Confidential Information received by it solely to its Representatives who have a need to know such in connection with any current or future negotiations or arrangements between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party, including, without limitation in connection with the membership of the Investor in the Company and any commercial arrangements between the Investor and the Company and only for use in connection therewith. The Investor may provide access to and may disclose Confidential Information of the Company to any other Member of the Company in connection with such Member's participation as a Member of the Company. The Disclosing Party hereby consents to, and represents and warrants that it has obtained from its applicable Affiliates their consent to the use by the Receiving Party of any information received by the Receiving Party in connection with the above-referenced negotiations or arrangements;
perturbation.changed_text: 2. CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS. Except as expressly authorized by prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall: (a) limit access to any Confidential Information received by it solely to its Representatives. The Investor may provide access to and may disclose Confidential Information of the Company to any other Member of the Company in connection with such Member's participation as a Member of the Company. The Disclosing Party hereby consents to, and represents and warrants that it has obtained from its applicable Affiliates their consent to the use by the Receiving Party of any information received by the Receiving Party in connection with the above-referenced negotiations or arrangements;
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the condition that access is limited to representatives who 'have a need to know' and the connection to 'current or future negotiations'. This contradicts the intention of confidentiality in the original agreement, where access was strictly controlled based on necessity. The contradiction happens with the remainder of Section 2, because without the 'need to know', it can be inferred, access can be unlimited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS. Except as expressly authorized by prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall: (a) limit access to any Confidential Information received by it solely to its Representatives. The Investor may provide access to and may disclose Confidential Information of the Company to any other Member of the Company in connection with such Member's participation as a Member of the Company. The Disclosing Party hereby consents to, and represents and warrants that it has obtained from its applicable Affiliates their consent to the use by the Receiving Party of any information received by the Receiving Party in connection with the above-referenced negotiations or arrangements; (b) advise its Representatives having access to the Confidential Information of the proprietary nature thereof and of the obligation set forth in this Agreement; (c) take appropriate action by instruction or agreement with its Representatives having access to the Confidential Information to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement; (d) safeguard all Confidential Information received by it using a reasonable degree of care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of such Confidential Information, but not less than that degree of care and discretion used by the Receiving Party in safeguarding its own similar information or material that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate; and (e) use all Confidential Information received by it for the purposes described in subsection (a) of this Section 2 and for no other purpose whatsoever. CONFIDENTIAL Page 28 <Page>
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 2 limits access to confidential information to representatives who 'have a need to know' in connection with negotiations, while the changed text removes this condition. This contradicts the remainder of Section 2 (the contradicted text), which outlines specific obligations regarding the safeguarding and use of confidential information. By removing the 'need to know' requirement, the changed text implies potentially unlimited access, which undermines the purpose of the confidentiality obligations in the remainder of the Section 2 (contradicted text) regarding advising representatives of proprietary nature and using the data for specific purposes.

perturbation.original_text: 3. DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION UPON REQUEST. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall (i) destroy all memoranda, notes, records, drawings, manuals, other documents or materials and any other manifestation (and all copies thereof) pertaining to or arising from the Confidential Information in any form or medium (whether written, typewritten, recorded, electronic or otherwise) and (ii) at the option of the Receiving Party, either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy the Confidential Information supplied by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives. Upon the return and/or destruction of such materials, the Receiving Party agrees to certify, in writing, that all of the foregoing materials have been destroyed or surrendered to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION UPON REQUEST. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall make reasonable efforts to (i) destroy all memoranda, notes, records, drawings, manuals, other documents or materials and any other manifestation (and all copies thereof) pertaining to or arising from the Confidential Information in any form or medium (whether written, typewritten, recorded, electronic or otherwise) and (ii) at the option of the Receiving Party, either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy the Confidential Information supplied by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives. Upon the return and/or destruction of such materials, the Receiving Party agrees to certify, in writing, that all of the foregoing materials have been destroyed or surrendered to the Disclosing Party only if they feel like it.
perturbation.explanation: This changes 'shall' to 'make reasonable efforts to' destroy confidential information and adds 'only if they feel like it' to the certification obligation, making the destruction and certification non-mandatory. This directly contradicts the purpose of Section 3, which is to ensure the secure destruction of confidential information upon request. Other clauses assume information needs to be returned or destroyed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION UPON REQUEST. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall make reasonable efforts to (i) destroy all memoranda, notes, records, drawings, manuals, other documents or materials and any other manifestation (and all copies thereof) pertaining to or arising from the Confidential Information in any form or medium (whether written, typewritten, recorded, electronic or otherwise) and (ii) at the option of the Receiving Party, either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy the Confidential Information supplied by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives. Upon the return and/or destruction of such materials, the Receiving Party agrees to certify, in writing, that all of the foregoing materials have been destroyed or surrendered to the Disclosing Party only if they feel like it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the obligation of the Receiving Party to destroy confidential information from 'shall' to 'make reasonable efforts to' and adds 'only if they feel like it' to the certification obligation. This introduces a significant ambiguity and makes the destruction and certification non-mandatory, directly conflicting with the purpose of ensuring the secure destruction of confidential information. The contradicted text section itself has the same language as the changed text, but the explanation clarifies that this change creates the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. EXCEPTIONS TO CONFIDENTIALITY. The obligation of confidentiality and restriction on use in Section 2 hereof shall not apply to any Confidential Information that the Receiving Party proves: (a) was in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement or subsequently came into the public domain other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party; (b) was received by the Receiving Party from a third party free of any obligation of confidence of the Receiving Party to such third party; provided that such third party is not known by the Receiving Party, after reasonable diligence, to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other obligation of secrecy to the Disclosing Party; (c) was already in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party, without obligation of confidentiality, prior to receipt thereof, directly or indirectly, from the Disclosing Party; (d) is requested to be disclosed in a judicial or administrative law, regulation or proceeding after all reasonable legal remedies for maintaining such information in confidence have been exhausted, including, but not limited to, giving the Disclosing Party reasonable advance notice of the potential for such disclosure and allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order concerning such disclosure; or (e) is subsequently and independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. EXCEPTIONS TO CONFIDENTIALITY. The obligation of confidentiality and restriction on use in Section 2 hereof shall not apply to any Confidential Information that the Receiving Party claims: (a) was in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement or subsequently came into the public domain; (b) was received by the Receiving Party from a third party; (c) was already in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party; (d) is requested to be disclosed in a judicial or administrative law, regulation or proceeding; or (e) is subsequently and independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the 'proof' requirement, the due diligence requirement for third parties, and other restrictive clauses, significantly broadening the exceptions to confidentiality. This introduces a contradiction with the fundamental purpose of the entire agreement, which aims to protect confidential information. The term 'claims' allows exceptions to be made without actual validation, while originally 'proves' needs it. The new list of exceptions contradicts every aspect of Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. EXCEPTIONS TO CONFIDENTIALITY. The obligation of confidentiality and restriction on use in Section 2 hereof shall not apply to any Confidential Information that the Receiving Party claims: (a) was in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement or subsequently came into the public domain; (b) was received by the Receiving Party from a third party; (c) was already in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party; (d) is requested to be disclosed in a judicial or administrative law, regulation or proceeding; or (e) is subsequently and independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly broadens the exceptions to confidentiality by removing the 'proof' requirement and other restrictive clauses present in the original text. This directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of the agreement, which is to protect confidential information, and creates a direct conflict as exceptions can be made based on mere 'claims' without validation. This makes the changed text incompatible with the original intent and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. RIGHTS IN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Except as specifically provided for herein, this Agreement does not confer any right, license, interest or title in, to or under the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the Receiving Party. No license is hereby granted to the Receiving Party, by estoppel or otherwise, under any patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary rights of the Disclosing Party. Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. RIGHTS IN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Except as specifically provided for herein, this Agreement does confer a temporary right to use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the Receiving Party, solely for reviewing and improving the agreement. No license is hereby granted to the Receiving Party, by estoppel or otherwise, under any patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary rights of the Disclosing Party. Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces that the agreement 'does confer a temporary right', contradicting the original statement that it does not. It creates confusion in Section 5, since one sentence says there is no right being conferred, while the immediate next states that it is confering the right for the Receiving Party to use the Confidential Information. This implies access and potential modification rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. RIGHTS IN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Except as specifically provided for herein, this Agreement does confer a temporary right to use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the Receiving Party, solely for reviewing and improving the agreement. No license is hereby granted to the Receiving Party, by estoppel or otherwise, under any patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary rights of the Disclosing Party. Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement 'does not confer any right'. The changed text modifies this to 'does confer a temporary right', directly contradicting the initial statement. This creates a clear and direct conflict within Section 5, as explained in the perturbation explanation, making the agreement internally inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 6. TERMINATION. This Agreement may not be terminated except following Withdrawal by the Investor pursuant to the terms of the LLC Agreement. The obligations of confidentiality and limitations on use under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the execution of this Agreement; PROVIDED, CONFIDENTIAL Page 29 <Page> HOWEVER, notwithstanding the term of this Agreement, any provisions hereof that by their nature extend beyond such term or termination shall remain in effect beyond such term or termination under this Section 6 until fulfilled and shall apply to either Party's successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. TERMINATION. This Agreement may be terminated at any time by either party with 30 days written notice. The obligations of confidentiality and limitations on use under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) year from the execution of this Agreement; PROVIDED, CONFIDENTIAL Page 29 <Page> HOWEVER, notwithstanding the term of this Agreement, any provisions hereof that by their nature extend beyond such term or termination shall remain in effect beyond such term or termination under this Section 6 until fulfilled and shall apply to either Party's successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the termination conditions and duration of confidentiality, introducing a conflict with the original intent. The new language directly contradicts the initial statement that termination is only possible after Withdrawal and that the period of confidentiality will be five (5) years, because it now says, it can be terminated at any time with 30 days written notice and the period of confidentiality will be one (1) year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. TERMINATION. This Agreement may be terminated at any time by either party with 30 days written notice. The obligations of confidentiality and limitations on use under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) year from the execution of this Agreement; PROVIDED, CONFIDENTIAL Page 29 <Page> HOWEVER, notwithstanding the term of this Agreement, any provisions hereof that by their nature extend beyond such term or termination shall remain in effect beyond such term or termination under this Section 6 until fulfilled and shall apply to either Party's successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions for termination and the duration of confidentiality. The original text states that termination is only possible following withdrawal, while the changed text allows termination at any time with 30 days' notice. Furthermore, the original text specifies a five-year confidentiality period, while the changed text reduces it to one year. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, leading to a clear and significant in-text contradiction as it alters fundamental aspects of the agreement, making the terms inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. The contradiction falls under the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition, due to the conflicting terms for termination and confidentiality.
